text: The Sleepcasts are absolutely incredible. I fall asleep to The Garden every night and I am out within 10 minutes. Nothing else has worked for my insomnia like this.
Sleep music selection is amazing but I wish there were more variety in the Sleepcasts. I have listened to The Garden so many times I have it memorized.
I downloaded Headspace specifically for the Sleepcasts and they do not disappoint. The ambient storytelling format is genius. Highly recommend for anyone with sleep issues.
Sleepcast audio quality is top notch but several of the sleep sounds cut out after 30 minutes even when I have the timer set to all night. Very frustrating.
The wind-down exercises before bed combined with a Sleepcast have completely transformed my sleep routine. I go to bed earlier now because I actually look forward to it.
I love the nighttime wind-down series. The breathing exercises paired with the gentle voice really help me decompress after stressful work days.
The sleep sounds stop playing when my phone locks which completely defeats the purpose. I need the rain sounds to play all night not just while my screen is on.
Downloaded for sleep and I stay for the meditation. The Sleepcasts gave me the best sleep I have had in years. The narration is calm and slow and perfectly paced.
Sleep music is lovely but I cannot download it for offline use without premium. My wifi is weak at night and the audio keeps buffering which ruins the experience.
The Dreamy playlist has completely replaced my old white noise machine. Way more soothing and the variety keeps my brain from fixating on patterns.
Really wanted to try the Sleepcasts but most of them are locked behind a paywall. The one free Sleepcast is great but I cannot justify the subscription just for sleep audio.
I have tried every sleep app and Headspace Sleepcasts are in a different league. The combination of ambient sounds and gentle narration is uniquely effective.
Sleep content is good but the app keeps sending notifications at 2am if I forget to turn on do not disturb. That is completely counterproductive.
The bedtime wind-down course helped me establish a consistent sleep schedule for the first time in my adult life. My sleep tracking shows real improvement over 30 days.
I appreciate the sleep content but some Sleepcasts feel too short and then there is silence which wakes me up. I need them to loop or fade into continuous ambient sound.
The Before Sleep mindfulness exercises help me stop ruminating at bedtime. Used to take 2 hours to fall asleep and now I am out in under 30 minutes consistently.
Sleep sounds timer does not work reliably. I set it for 60 minutes and sometimes it stops after 20 minutes and sometimes it plays until morning. No consistency.
The Ocean Sleepcast is my absolute favourite. I can visualise the scene they describe and my brain just switches off. It feels like a bedtime story for adults.
Headspace has helped my teenage daughter who has severe anxiety fall asleep without medication. The calming voice and sleep exercises make such a difference.
I wish there were new Sleepcasts added more frequently. I have been a subscriber for 8 months and the same ones keep getting recycled. Content feels stale now.
The sleep sounds are beautiful but the app plays ads between tracks even though I am a paying subscriber. That is completely unacceptable and ruins the sleep experience.
Absolutely love the sleep section. The way the Sleepcasts slowly reduce the narration and let the ambient audio take over is perfectly designed to mirror the falling asleep process.
The sleep content helped with my shift-work sleep disorder. Being able to choose Sleepcasts by environment like forest or ocean helps me associate the sounds with sleep.
I cannot access the full Sleepcast library without premium but even the free version gave me enough to see that this approach works for me. Converted me to a subscriber.
App crashes every single time I open a Sleepcast on my Samsung Galaxy. I have reinstalled it three times and the problem persists. Sleep content is inaccessible to me.
The mix of sleep music and body scan exercises in the Sleep course is exactly what I needed. Progressive muscle relaxation combined with ambient music is surprisingly effective.
Headspace made me realise I was going to bed anxious every night. The evening wind-down meditations specifically address the anxious thoughts that keep me awake.
The sleep stories and Sleepcasts are great but I do not want a monthly subscription just for those. Would love a la carte purchasing of specific content.
My doctor recommended Headspace for my insomnia and after two weeks of using the Sleepcasts I reduced my sleep medication dosage. Genuine therapeutic benefit.
Background audio keeps stopping when I receive a text message at night. The sound should not be interrupted by notifications when I am using it for sleep.
The Forest Rain Sleepcast is pure magic. I grew up near forests and the sound design is so accurate it transports me immediately. Fall asleep in minutes every time.
I use the sleep sounds during the day too when I need to focus. The brown noise option specifically helps with my ADHD concentration. Versatile audio library.
The Sleepcasts narration speed is too fast for me. When I am trying to fall asleep I need slower pacing. Wish there was a playback speed control for sleep content.
I have insomnia related to anxiety and the Sleepcasts combined with the SOS meditations have genuinely changed my relationship with sleep. Life-changing app.
Some sleep content requires an internet connection even after I downloaded it. The offline functionality for sleep content is fundamentally broken.
The night sounds collection is excellent but I want the ability to mix multiple sounds together like rain plus fire crackling. Would make the experience much richer.
As a nurse who works night shifts the Sleepcasts help me sleep during the day. The blackout sleep mode that disables the screen automatically is a thoughtful feature.
Premium sleep content is genuinely worth the subscription alone. Nothing on YouTube or Spotify comes close to the quality of Headspace Sleepcasts.
Sleep sounds stop when a call comes in and do not resume automatically. Minor thing but when you are almost asleep having to reopen the app is incredibly annoying.
The 30-day sleep course is comprehensive. It teaches sleep hygiene habits alongside the mindfulness practices and I now understand why I was sleeping poorly for years.
Andy Puddicombe is a gifted teacher. His voice and pacing in the guided meditations make even complex mindfulness concepts feel accessible and natural.
The 10-day Basics course completely changed my understanding of meditation. I thought it was about clearing your mind and Andy explained it is about observing thoughts. Revolutionary.
I completed the 30-day Stress course and my anxiety levels are measurably lower. My therapist noticed the change before I told her I was using Headspace.
The mini meditations are perfect for my schedule. A 3-minute session between meetings keeps me grounded without requiring a large time commitment.
I love the structured course format but I wish I could jump between courses more freely. The linear progression feels too rigid when I want to explore specific topics.
The SOS meditation is a lifesaver during panic attacks. Having a guided emergency meditation available in three taps has helped me manage my anxiety disorder significantly.
Andy voice is soothing but after two years of using Headspace I am bored of it. There is not enough variety in the guest teacher sessions to keep things fresh.
The Focus meditation series helped me get through my exams. Studying with the focus music and starting each session with a 5-minute meditation doubled my retention.
Basics 1 is free and it is excellent. After completing it I immediately subscribed because I could see exactly how the full course library would benefit me.
The meditation for sports performance improved my golf game significantly. The visualisation exercises and pre-round mindfulness routine reduced my first-tee nerves.
Headspace for Work has transformed our team meetings. We start with a 2-minute breathing exercise and the group focus and communication has noticeably improved.
The courses are well-structured but the daily recommendation algorithm keeps pushing the same beginner content even after I finished advanced courses. Needs smarter personalisation.
I am a psychologist and I recommend Headspace to clients as a complement to therapy. The evidence-based techniques are presented accurately and accessibly.
The 10-day Managing Anxiety course was more helpful than I expected. The metaphors Andy uses to explain anxiety responses clicked for me in ways that therapy never quite did.
Meditation streak feature is motivating but I lost my 47-day streak due to a server error and Headspace refused to restore it. Very demotivating.
The guest teacher packs are fantastic. Having different voices and approaches within the same app adds real variety without needing multiple apps.
I appreciate that each meditation session explains the technique being used and why it works. The educational approach makes me more committed to practicing.
The progress tracking for courses is satisfying but the percentage circle disappears sometimes and I lose track of where I am in longer courses.
The compassion and kindness meditations have made me a noticeably more patient parent. My kids have even started asking me to do my Headspace so I calm down.
Andy Puddicombe former Buddhist monk background gives the teachings real authenticity. It is not a gimmick it is genuine mindfulness wisdom made accessible.
The Move and Meditate sessions are brilliant for combining physical and mental wellness. A short yoga flow followed by meditation is now my morning ritual.
The meditation timer with ambient bells is perfectly calibrated. The gentle beginning and closing bells are non-jarring and help signal the start and end of practice.
I completed every course in the library and now feel like I have outgrown the app. Need more advanced content for long-term practitioners beyond the current offerings.
The Pregnancy meditation course was a wonderful companion through my third trimester. Breathing exercises for labour and anxiety management for new parenthood.
Headspace helped me through chemotherapy. Having a structured mindfulness practice during treatment gave me a sense of agency during a frightening time.
The eating well meditation series helped me address my emotional eating. Mindful eating is a concept I understood intellectually but Headspace helped me practice it.
I wish unguided meditation sessions with just a timer and ambient sounds were easier to find. The fully guided sessions are great for beginners but I want more independence.
The daily mindfulness reminders are helpful but I cannot customise the timing precisely enough. I need reminders at very specific times due to my work schedule.
Andy voice is incredibly calming but I want options for different accents or voices. Some users might connect better with someone who sounds like them.
The Focus music playlists have become essential for my work. The instrumental tracks designed to reduce cognitive distraction are backed by real neuroscience research.
Completed the 30-day Happiness course and while I was sceptical going in I genuinely feel different. The practical exercises for gratitude and reframing have stuck.
The session history shows how many minutes I have meditated which is motivating. Passed 1000 minutes last week and I feel like I actually know how to meditate now.
I tried many meditation apps and Headspace is by far the most pedagogically sound. It builds skills progressively rather than just giving you random guided sessions.
The relationship meditation pack helped my partner and I communicate better during a difficult period. We listened to the same sessions and discussed them together.
I want to be able to set different session lengths within a course. Sometimes I only have 5 minutes but the course session is 15 minutes and I cannot do a shorter version.
The visual animations that accompany some meditations are beautiful and help me visualise the concepts without being distracting. Really thoughtful design.
Group meditation sessions through Headspace for Work are excellent for team building. We use them in our monthly all-hands to create a reflective moment before business.
The sport specific packs for running and cycling help me enter a flow state during training. Using Headspace before competitions has improved my performance.
I am 67 years old and was hesitant about meditation apps. My granddaughter showed me Headspace and the simple interface and Andy patient voice made it approachable for me.
The courses are excellent but the order in which they are surfaced to me does not always match where I am in my practice. Better recommendation logic would help.
12.99 a month is too expensive for what is essentially an audio app. The content does not update frequently enough to justify the recurring cost month after month.
The annual subscription at 69.99 works out to under 6 dollars a month which is reasonable for the quality and breadth of content. Annual is the only sensible plan.
I cancelled because the price went up by 2 dollars per month with no notice and no new major content was added. Classic subscription creep.
Student discount was easy to apply and brings it to a price that makes sense for the value. I wish the discount lasted beyond one year for multi-year degree programmes.
The free tier is basically a demo that shows you what you are missing. 10 sessions and then a paywall. I understand the model but it feels too restrictive.
Family plan covers 6 members at a reasonable price. Got my whole household meditating. Value per person is excellent compared to individual subscriptions.
They charged me twice in the same billing cycle and customer service took three weeks to issue a refund. Billing system clearly has problems.
I spent 12.99 for a month to try it and it was worth it. Then I bought the annual plan. The free trial period should be longer than just the 10 basics sessions.
Cancelled subscription and they made it incredibly difficult to do so. Had to google how to cancel because there is no clear cancel option in the app settings.
The subscription auto-renewed after I thought I cancelled. I had to dispute the charge with my bank. The cancellation process is deliberately obfuscated.
Value is excellent if you actually use the app regularly. At 69.99 per year that is less than 20 cents per day for something that genuinely improves mental health.
I got Headspace through my employer wellness programme so it is free for me. Knowing the employer pays makes me more likely to use it consistently.
The price is fair but I want a lifetime purchase option. I do not want an ongoing subscription commitment. Pay once and access everything forever please.
My insurance covers Headspace as a mental health benefit. The reimbursement process was smooth. More insurers should cover wellness apps like this.
The Black Friday discount brought the annual plan down to 39.99 which is exceptional value. I wish they ran that kind of deal more than once a year.
I subscribed on iOS and cannot access my subscription on Android without re-subscribing through Google Play. Cross-platform subscription transfers should be seamless.
The content-to-price ratio is reasonable but I want to see monthly new content additions explicitly listed. It is hard to tell if new things are being added.
Free plan gives access to the Focus music which is surprisingly useful. More of the library should be free to encourage long-term subscription conversion.
I subscribed for 12.99 a month and used it for 3 months but found the content repetitive. Should assess how frequently you will use it before subscribing monthly.
The price increase email was unprofessional. They buried the price change in a newsletter rather than sending a dedicated clear notification. Found out when my card was charged.
I appreciate that they offer a reduced price for people in lower income countries. Mental health tools should not be exclusively available to wealthy users.
The gift subscription feature is great for giving to friends and family. Bought my mother a year subscription for her birthday and she loves it.
Refund request after accidental purchase was denied even within the return window. Google Play policy override should not mean Headspace ignores basic consumer rights.
Worth every penny of the annual subscription. The mental health value of consistent meditation practice is impossible to put a price on.
Three day free trial is not enough time to evaluate the full library and decide if the subscription is worth it. Should be at least two weeks.
I switched from another meditation app because Headspace annual plan is cheaper and the content quality is higher. Best value in the meditation app category.
Subscription price seems reasonable until you realise most of the genuinely good content has been locked in a way that requires the premium tier.
Got a promo code through my university and the discounted rate has kept me subscribing for three years. Loyalty discounts would help retain long-term subscribers.
Fine with paying but I want to be able to buy individual courses I am interested in rather than paying for the entire library which I mostly do not use.
The annual plan is good value but monthly plan is not. If you are considering Headspace go straight to annual or wait for a promotional period.
Charged for a subscription after the trial without a reminder email. The opt-out reminder before billing should be mandatory not optional.
My company pays for the team plan. Headspace for Work pricing is competitive compared to other corporate wellness solutions and the content is superior.
I paused my subscription during a difficult financial period and they allowed it without cancelling. That kind of flexibility builds real customer loyalty.
The pricing comparison to a single therapy session puts it in perspective. One session with a therapist costs more than a year of Headspace. Incredible value at that scale.
Getting a refund is impossible. Their customer service told me to contact Google Play and Google Play told me to contact Headspace. I am stuck in a loop with 35 dollars lost.
I downgraded from premium to free mid-cycle and they did not pro-rate refund the remaining days. Only found out after when I saw no credit applied.
The student tier verification through SheerID is seamless and the discount is meaningful. Accessible pricing for students is important for building lifelong habits.
12.99 a month for an app I use for 10 minutes a day is not great value unless you compare it to the prescription anxiety medication it partially replaced for me.
I paused thinking about the monthly cost and a friend sent me a 30-day trial code. That trial period was enough to convince me the annual subscription is worthwhile.
Good app but the paywall hits so fast that new users cannot properly evaluate the product before committing. Needs a more generous free tier to build trust first.
The app crashes every time I try to download sessions for offline use on my Pixel 8. Reported to support weeks ago and still no fix. Useless without offline access.
Background audio stops playing the moment I switch to another app on Android 14. This is a known issue that has not been fixed for months. Complete deal breaker.
The app is slow to load on older Android devices. Takes nearly 30 seconds to open which disrupts my morning routine. Needs significant performance optimisations.
My meditation streak reset to zero after a server outage that was not my fault. I had a 92-day streak and Headspace support told me they cannot restore it manually.
Cast to Chromecast feature never works properly. Audio stutters constantly when casting to my TV speakers. Have to use my phone speaker instead which is not ideal.
Google account sign-in is broken on the latest app version. I cannot log in at all. Had to reset to an older APK to access my account and purchased content.
Bluetooth headphone connection drops mid-session regularly. The audio pauses and takes 5 to 10 seconds to reconnect which completely breaks the mindfulness experience.
The app logs me out randomly and I lose my progress data. This has happened four times in the past two months. My session history keeps disappearing.
Push notifications arrive hours late or not at all. My daily meditation reminder is set for 7am but it arrives at 2pm or sometimes not until the next day.
Downloaded content does not actually play offline. The app says sessions are downloaded but then throws an internet required error when I try to play them in airplane mode.
The animation that plays while a session loads freezes the entire app. I have to force close and reopen it to get past the loading screen on my Samsung.
Audio quality degrades when battery saver mode is active. The meditation voices sound distorted and compressed. This should not be affected by power management settings.
App stopped working after the latest Android system update. Crashes on launch with no error message. Uninstalled and reinstalled and the problem persists.
The sleep timer does not work correctly. I set it for 45 minutes and the audio stops after exactly 12 minutes every time. Completely unreliable for sleep use.
Widget on home screen stopped updating my streak counter and daily session status. Minor bug but annoying to see outdated information every morning.
Error 403 when trying to access the account page. I cannot update my payment information and my subscription is about to expire. No way to fix this in the app.
The app does not remember my last position in a long session. If I am interrupted and close the app it restarts from the beginning next time. Needs resume from position.
Force closes when I try to access the profile page on Android 13. All my mindfulness data is inaccessible because I cannot reach my stats without crashing the app.
Volume controls inside the app do not sync with system volume. I have to use both the app slider and the hardware volume buttons which is unnecessarily complicated.
Session timer counts down at the wrong speed. A 10-minute session shows 10 minutes remaining but ends after 7 minutes actual time. Clock is consistently wrong.
The dark mode implementation makes several text elements unreadable with dark text on dark backgrounds. This is a basic UI oversight that should have been caught in testing.
Purchases made on the web version do not appear in the Android app. I have to re-enter my credentials every time I switch between web and mobile. Sync is broken.
In-app audio equaliser settings reset to default after every session. I have to re-adjust bass and treble every single time I open the app. Very tedious.
The morning reminder notification when tapped opens the home screen instead of the recommended session. A notification that deep-links to the content would be far more useful.
App crashes when I try to rate a session after completing it. I cannot leave feedback on any session because the rating modal causes an immediate force close.
The search function within the app is very poor. Searching for specific topics like grief or anger returns irrelevant results and misses obviously relevant content.
Headspace broke something in its last update that makes the app run at double speed on my device. Andy sounds like a chipmunk and sessions end in half the expected time.
Account data did not transfer when I upgraded my phone. Lost two years of meditation history and streaks. There should be cloud backup that handles device changes automatically.
Cannot cast session audio to my Bluetooth speaker reliably. The connection drops every 10 minutes and the session pauses. Have given up trying to use my speaker.
The app uses an unreasonable amount of mobile data even when playing downloaded content. Background data usage appears to be uncapped and drains my monthly allowance.
Profile picture upload always fails with a generic error. This is a cosmetic issue but it shows that basic features are not properly tested before release.
There is no landscape mode support on tablets. Using Headspace on my Tab S9 in landscape is impossible because the UI is stuck in portrait orientation.
The guided session audio and background music play at very different volume levels. The music is too loud relative to Andy voice and there is no separate mix control.
App took 12 minutes to sync my progress after I reinstalled on a new phone. The progress sync speed is unacceptably slow and shows the backend is not optimised.
The offline download queue gets stuck at 99 per cent and never completes. Have tried on three different WiFi networks with the same result. Bug has been present for months.
Screen recording breaks after a Headspace session ends. I do not know what the app is doing to my device permissions but it interferes with other apps.
The buddy feature for checking friends meditation status loads a blank screen. Feature appears broken entirely. Should be removed from the UI if it does not work.
Alarm integration that was supposed to create a morning meditation start routine has never worked for me. The app does not respond to the alarm trigger.
Cannot use the app on my work phone due to MDM policy conflicts. Would be great if there was a web app version with full functionality for corporate device users.
Play and pause button in the notification bar occasionally pauses and immediately resumes like it receives two tap events from one tap. Very glitchy media control.
The mood check-in at the start of each session is a great feature but the recommendations it generates do not seem to actually respond to what I tell it. Feels like theatre.
I want the app to learn my preferences over time. After two years it still recommends basics courses I finished in month one. The personalisation is essentially static.
The breadth of content covering anger management grief bereavement relationships and physical health is impressive. This is a genuinely comprehensive mental wellness library.
I meditate in Spanish but the Headspace Spanish language library is a fraction of the English content. Either match the language libraries or stop advertising full multilingual support.
The animations and illustrations throughout the app are delightful. The art style is distinctive and makes the somewhat abstract concepts of mindfulness feel warm and approachable.
Content for children is excellent. My 8 year old daughter uses the kids section independently and it has helped her manage her emotions at school.
I have been a subscriber for three years and the content library has not grown significantly in the last 18 months. Feels like the team has stopped investing in new material.
The curated daily recommendation is usually spot-on for my current needs. Somehow the algorithm picks content that feels relevant even on days I did not specify my mood.
Would love more content specifically for chronic pain management. The existing pain content is helpful but limited to a few sessions that I have already exhausted.
The cultural representation in the content is improving but still primarily reflects Western mindfulness perspectives. Would value more teachers from South Asian and East Asian traditions.
Headspace for Kids is a hidden gem. The animations are engaging and the meditation concepts are explained in genuinely child-friendly language not just dumbed-down adult content.
The productivity and focus section has more content than any other category which makes sense but I wish the relationships and grief sections had equal depth.
I want to be able to mark sessions as not for me without it affecting my overall recommendations. Currently skipping content seems to reset my entire recommendation history.
The app does an excellent job surfacing seasonal content around major life events like back to school stress or holiday anxiety. Timely recommendations feel thoughtful.
Content variety is good but the sessions within each category often feel repetitive in their structure and approach. Different teaching styles for different learning types would help.
The journal feature for reflecting after sessions is underused and undersold. It is a powerful complement to meditation that I only discovered after 6 months of using the app.
I want more content for the workplace beyond the existing productivity content. Team conflict resolution interpersonal communication and leadership mindfulness would be valuable.
The library filter options are not intuitive enough. Finding content by duration teacher or specific topic requires too many taps compared to a simple search and filter interface.
The outdoor walking meditation series is a rare combination of movement and mindfulness that no other app does as well. The nature sound design during walking sessions is beautiful.
I want the app to sync with my fitness tracker data so it can suggest restorative content when I have had an intense workout week. Data integration would make recommendations smarter.
Content for ADHD is surprisingly good. The focus techniques acknowledged my specific challenges rather than treating me like someone who just needs to try harder to concentrate.
The beginner and advanced content are clearly differentiated but the intermediate zone is underdeveloped. There is a significant jump in assumption between the two levels.
I appreciate that new guest teachers are being added but I want the ability to favourite specific teachers and get notified when they release new content.
The menstrual cycle mindfulness content was unexpected and genuinely useful. Tailoring meditation practice to hormonal phases is an underexplored area that Headspace handles sensitively.
I want to create my own playlists by combining sessions from different courses. The inability to build customised sequences forces me to use workarounds.
The content tagging and organisation has improved but still needs work. Sessions tagged as beginner friendly sometimes assume knowledge that beginners would not have.
The grief and loss course was written with extraordinary sensitivity. I used it after losing my father and felt genuinely held by the content in a difficult time.
Headspace added a cooking mindfulness series which I loved. Expanding mindfulness into everyday activities beyond sitting meditation makes the practice feel more integrated.
The recommendation algorithm strongly favours content I have not tried over content I want to revisit. I want to be able to schedule specific favourite sessions as recurring practices.
Post-session reflection prompts in the journal are thoughtful and push my thinking beyond the session itself. The combination of guided meditation and reflective journaling is powerful.
I want integration with Apple Health and Google Fit so my meditation minutes count toward the health and mindfulness goals tracked across my devices.
The curated collections for specific life situations like new parent or starting a new job or moving to a new city are brilliant. Contextual content bundles are exactly what I need.
Diversity of content has expanded noticeably this year. The addition of teachers with different backgrounds and approaches makes the library feel more representative and inclusive.
Personalization is too shallow. Answering a 4-question onboarding quiz should not define my recommendations for the next year. Regular preference updates should be built into the experience.
The social features that let you see friend activity are minimal compared to fitness apps. A community practice element would add accountability that keeps people engaged.
Content for parents meditating with young children is brilliant. The joint parent-child sessions we do every Sunday morning have become a family tradition.
I want a way to request content topics. There is no feedback mechanism for suggesting what is missing from the library. User-driven content development would improve relevance.
The commuter meditation series designed to be used in noisy public spaces is genuinely clever design. The open eye technique and noise-tolerant approach works in the real world.
Headspace content has helped me understand that mindfulness is a skill not a talent. The progressive course structure made it clear that improvement comes from practice not aptitude.
The library is wide but I want it deeper. More sessions per topic would let me go further with specific practices rather than having to repeat the same 5 sessions endlessly.
